items.verse: ۞ قُلْ أَؤُنَبِّئُكُم بِخَيْرٍۢ مِّن ذَٰلِكُمْ ۚ لِلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّـٰتٌۭ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا وَأَزْوَٰجٌۭ مُّطَهَّرَةٌۭ وَرِضْوَٰنٌۭ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بَصِيرٌۢ بِٱلْعِبَادِ
items.verseEn: Say, "Shall I inform you of [something] better than that? For those who fear Allāh will be gardens in the presence of their Lord beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally, and purified spouses and approval from Allāh. And Allāh is Seeing [i.e., aware] of [His] servants -

items.verse: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُدْخِلُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ جَنَّـٰتٍۢ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَفْعَلُ مَا يُرِيدُ
items.verseEn: Indeed, Allāh will admit those who believe and do righteous deeds to gardens beneath which rivers flow. Indeed, Allāh does what He intends.
Within it are couches raised high

categories.name: أَلْفَاظٌ زَمَنِيَّةٌ
categories.nameEn: Terms for Time Periods in the Quran

items.verse: ۞ يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلْأَهِلَّةِ ۖ قُلْ هِىَ مَوَٰقِيتُ لِلنَّاسِ وَٱلْحَجِّ ۗ وَلَيْسَ ٱلْبِرُّ بِأَن تَأْتُوا۟ ٱلْبُيُوتَ مِن ظُهُورِهَا وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلْبِرَّ مَنِ ٱتَّقَىٰ ۗ وَأْتُوا۟ ٱلْبُيُوتَ مِنْ أَبْوَٰبِهَا ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
items.verseEn: They ask you, [O Muḥammad], about the crescent moons. Say, "They are measurements of time for the people and for ḥajj [pilgrimage]." And it is not righteousness to enter houses from the back, but righteousness is [in] one who fears Allāh. And enter houses from their doors. And fear Allāh that you may succeed.

items.verse: إِلَّا رَحْمَةًۭ مِّنَّا وَمَتَـٰعًا إِلَىٰ حِينٍۢ
items.verseEn: Except as a mercy from Us and provision for a time.

items.verse: ۞ أَلَمْ يَأْنِ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَن تَخْشَعَ قُلُوبُهُمْ لِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ وَمَا نَزَلَ مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ وَلَا يَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِن قَبْلُ فَطَالَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْأَمَدُ فَقَسَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌۭ مِّنْهُمْ فَـٰسِقُونَ
items.verseEn: Has the time not come for those who have believed that their hearts should become humbly submissive at the remembrance of Allāh and what has come down of the truth? And let them not be like those who were given the Scripture before, and a long period passed over them, so their hearts hardened; and many of them are defiantly disobedient.

items.verse: هَلْ أَتَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْإِنسَـٰنِ حِينٌۭ مِّنَ ٱلدَّهْرِ لَمْ يَكُن شَيْـًۭٔا مَّذْكُورًا
items.verseEn: Has there [not] come upon man a period of time when he was not a thing [even] mentioned?
They will [enter to] burn in an intensely hot Fire.

items.verse: وَمَن لَّمْ يُؤْمِنۢ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ فَإِنَّآ أَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ سَعِيرًۭا
items.verseEn: And whoever has not believed in Allāh and His Messenger - then indeed, We have prepared for the disbelievers a Blaze.
They will circulate between it and scalding water, heated [to the utmost degree].
And Hellfire will be brought forth for the deviators,

items.verse: رَسُولٌۭ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ يَتْلُوا۟ صُحُفًۭا مُّطَهَّرَةًۭ
items.verseEn: A Messenger from Allāh, reciting purified scriptures

items.verse: يَوْمَ نَطْوِى ٱلسَّمَآءَ كَطَىِّ ٱلسِّجِلِّ لِلْكُتُبِ ۚ كَمَا بَدَأْنَآ أَوَّلَ خَلْقٍۢ نُّعِيدُهُۥ ۚ وَعْدًا عَلَيْنَآ ۚ إِنَّا كُنَّا فَـٰعِلِينَ
items.verseEn: The Day when We will fold the heaven like the folding of a [written] sheet for the records. As We began the first creation, We will repeat it. [That is] a promise binding upon Us. Indeed, We will do it.
It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed

harf.hint: A plea made on someone else's behalf — only of value when permitted by the One in whose court it is made
harf.arabicVerse: وَلَا تَنفَعُ ٱلشَّفَـٰعَةُ عِندَهُۥٓ إِلَّا لِمَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُۥ ۚ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا فُزِّعَ عَن قُلُوبِهِمْ قَالُوا۟ مَاذَا قَالَ رَبُّكُمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ ٱلْحَقَّ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْكَبِيرُ
harf.verse: 34:23 - And intercession does not benefit with Him except for one whom He permits. [And those wait] until, when terror is removed from their hearts, they will say [to one another], "What has your Lord said?" They will say, "The truth." And He is the Most High, the Grand.

deduction.title: The surrendered soul of sacred flame
deduction.intro: Before you lies a tale of extraordinary courage, where one individual dared to challenge the most entrenched beliefs of an entire civilization. Follow these whispers from the past, and you will uncover a life marked by unyielding faith, miraculous survival, and the laying of foundations that would shape the spiritual journey of billions. Trace each clue carefully — the sacred fire still remembers.
clues: I was born into a household that revered celestial bodies and carved idols, yet something deep within me rejected what they worshipped.
I stood against the mightiest ruler of my land, shattering the very idols my people bowed to, and declaring that only the Creator of the heavens deserved devotion.
A blazing pyre was ordered in my name, but I proclaimed that the One I served alone would save me — and the flames were commanded to become cool and safe.
I left behind everything familiar on a divine command, wandering through arid valleys with my family, trusting in a provision I could not yet see.
Standing beside my firstborn son in a barren valley, we raised together the foundations of a sacred house, a sanctuary destined for all who submit.
I am known as Khalilullah — the intimate friend of the Most Merciful — and my name became forever linked with the faith of surrender and submission.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham)
Prophet Musa (Moses)
Prophet Nuh (Noah)
Prophet Isa (Jesus)
Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Being thrown into a blazing fire by a tyrant
Being swallowed inside a great fish at sea
Being lowered into a pit by jealous brothers
Being carried aloft upon a winged creature
Enduring a great flood inside a vessel of wood
trial.answer: Being thrown into a blazing fire by a tyrant

location.label: Key Element
options: The Sacred House (Kaaba)
A great wooden vessel built upon a mount
A staff that could split open the sea
A cave of long slumber with a loyal hound
A scroll of revealed law bestowed upon a mount

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Became known as Khalilullah, the intimate friend of Allah
Led a people across a parted sea to safety
Was granted mastery over winds and unseen forces
Became a healer blessed with miraculous cures
United twelve scattered tribes under one covenant
outcome.answer: Became known as Khalilullah, the intimate friend of Allah

deduction.arabic: إِنِّى وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِىَ لِلَّذِى فَطَرَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ حَنِيفًۭا ۖ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ
deduction.verse: Indeed, I have turned my face [i.e., self] toward He who created the heavens and the earth, inclining toward truth, and I am not of those who associate others with Allāh." (6:79)

translations: Then when they have reached their term then retain them with kindness or part with them with kindness
And take witness two men just among you and establish the testimony for Allah That
is instructed with it whoever [is] believes in Allah and the Day
the Last And whoever fears Allah He will make for him a way out
scramble.hint: This verse provides guidance about maintaining marital relations while observing oaths, and reminds us of Allah's promise to ease difficulties for the pious.
scramble.arabic: فَإِذَا بَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ فَارِقُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍۢ وَأَشْهِدُوا۟ ذَوَىْ عَدْلٍۢ مِّنكُمْ وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلشَّهَـٰدَةَ لِلَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ يُوعَظُ بِهِۦ مَن كَانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُۥ مَخْرَجًۭا
words: فَإِذَا بَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ فَارِقُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍۢ
وَأَشْهِدُوا۟ ذَوَىْ عَدْلٍۢ مِّنكُمْ وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلشَّهَـٰدَةَ لِلَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ
يُوعَظُ بِهِۦ مَن كَانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ
ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ يَجْعَل لَّهُۥ مَخْرَجًۭا
scramble.verseEn: And when they have [nearly] fulfilled their term, either retain them according to acceptable terms or part with them according to acceptable terms. And bring to witness two just men from among you and establish the testimony for [the acceptance of] Allāh. That is instructed to whoever should believe in Allāh and the Last Day. And whoever fears Allāh - He will make for him a way out
